claimText: SPArc (Step-and-Shoot Spot-Scanning Proton Arc Therapy) is an advanced radiation therapy technique that targets tumors with protons while sparing surrounding healthy tissue.
evidenceExcerpt: It works by targeting tumours with protons while sparing surrounding tissue.
examUse: Statement-based question on the mechanism and benefits of SPArc.
distractorHooks: Uses electrons or X-rays instead of protons

claimText: SPArc functions by damaging the DNA of cancer cells.
evidenceExcerpt: SPArc is an advanced radiation therapy technique that damages cancer cells' DNA.
examUse: Statement-based question on how proton arc therapy destroys cancer cells.

claimText: Adenoid Cystic Carcinoma (ACC) is a rare form of cancer that can be treated using SPArc.
evidenceExcerpt: For the first time, SPArc has been used to treat an Adenoid Cystic Carcinoma (ACC), a relatively rare form of cancer.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on recent medical applications of SPArc.
distractorHooks: ACC is a common bacterial infection
SPArc is used exclusively for blood cancers like leukemia
